subject: Computer Science
instructions: Answer all questions. Choose the most appropriate option for each question. Each question carries 2.5 marks.

questions.question: A networking system is best described as
questions.B: b) interconnected devices sharing data and resources

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a Peer-to-Peer network model?
questions.B: b) Dedicated servers for resource sharing
questions.C: c) No centralized control over resources

questions.question: The OSI model consists of how many layers?
Which layer of the OSI model is responsible for routing data packets across networks?
What is the primary function of the Transport Layer (Layer 4) in the OSI model?
questions.A: a) Error detection and correction at the bit level
questions.B: b) End-to-end communication and data flow control
questions.C: c) Physical addressing using MAC addresses
questions.D: d) Converting data into signals for transmission

questions.question: Which protocol is responsible for resolving IP addresses to MAC addresses?
What type of IP address is 192.168.1.10?
Which of the following network devices operates at the Data Link Layer (Layer 2) and forwards data based on MAC addresses?
A subnet mask of 255.255.255.0 is equivalent to a CIDR notation of
Which protocol is used to automatically assign IP addresses to devices on a network?
What is the purpose of the Domain Name System (DNS)?
questions.A: a) To convert domain names to IP addresses
questions.B: b) To encrypt data for secure transmission

questions.question: Which of the following is a connectionless protocol at the Transport Layer?
A network topology where all devices are connected to a central device (like a switch) is called
Which of the following is a security threat that involves intercepting and altering communication between two parties without their knowledge?
What is the maximum data transfer rate of a standard Category 5e (Cat5e) Ethernet cable?
Which IEEE standard defines Wireless LAN (Wi-Fi) networking?
The process of breaking data into smaller units for transmission over a network is known as
Which of the following is a valid IPv6 address?
What is the role of a firewall in a network?
questions.A: a) To amplify signals for long-distance transmission
questions.B: b) To monitor and control incoming and outgoing network traffic based on security rules
questions.C: c) To assign IP addresses to devices

questions.question: In the TCP/IP model, which layer is equivalent to the combination of the Application, Presentation, and Session layers of the OSI model?
